name: New Age Specializations
description: An innovative program covering emerging technologies including quantum computing, edge computing, AR/VR, blockchain, DevOps, SRE, and other cutting-edge fields shaping the future of technology.

subjects.name: Quantum Computing
subjects.description: Introduction to quantum computing principles including qubits, quantum gates, quantum circuits, superposition, entanglement, and quantum algorithms like Shor's and Grover's algorithms.
learning_outcomes: Explain quantum superposition and entanglement principles
Design quantum circuits using basic quantum gates
Implement quantum algorithms including Deutsch-Jozsa and Grover's search
Program quantum computers using Qiskit or Cirq frameworks
subjects.industry_relevance: Quantum computing promises breakthroughs in cryptography, drug discovery, optimization, and materials science with investment from Google, IBM, and Microsoft.

subjects.name: Edge Computing
subjects.description: Study of edge computing architectures that bring computation and data storage closer to data sources. Covers edge devices, fog computing, latency optimization, and edge AI deployment.
learning_outcomes: Design edge computing architectures for low-latency applications
Deploy machine learning models on edge devices
Implement fog computing layers for distributed processing
Optimize applications for resource-constrained edge environments
subjects.industry_relevance: Edge computing is critical for autonomous vehicles, industrial IoT, smart cities, and real-time analytics applications.

subjects.name: 5G Technology
subjects.description: Study of fifth-generation wireless technology covering 5G architecture, mmWave communications, massive MIMO, network slicing, beamforming, and 5G use cases in IoT and autonomous systems.
learning_outcomes: Explain 5G network architecture and protocol stack
Analyze massive MIMO and beamforming techniques
Design network slicing for diverse service requirements
Evaluate 5G performance characteristics including latency and throughput
subjects.industry_relevance: 5G is transforming telecommunications enabling autonomous vehicles, smart factories, telemedicine, and immersive experiences.

subjects.name: Augmented Reality
subjects.description: Fundamentals of augmented reality including display technologies, tracking systems, spatial mapping, AR content creation, and development using ARKit, ARCore, and web-based AR frameworks.
learning_outcomes: Implement marker-based and markerless AR tracking systems
Develop AR applications using ARKit and ARCore
Create interactive AR experiences with spatial mapping and occlusion
Deploy web-based AR solutions using WebXR
subjects.industry_relevance: AR is transforming retail, manufacturing, healthcare, education, and maintenance with overlaid digital information.

subjects.name: Virtual Reality
subjects.description: Immersive virtual reality technologies covering VR hardware, stereoscopic rendering, head tracking, interaction design, 3D audio, and VR development using Unity and Unreal Engine.
learning_outcomes: Develop VR applications using Unity 3D with SteamVR or Oculus SDK
Implement stereoscopic rendering and optical distortion correction
Design natural user interactions using hand tracking and controllers
Optimize VR experiences for performance and comfort
subjects.industry_relevance: VR is revolutionizing gaming, training simulations, architectural visualization, therapy, and remote collaboration.

subjects.name: Blockchain Technology
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of blockchain technology including distributed ledgers, consensus algorithms, smart contract development, dApps, tokenomics, and enterprise blockchain platforms like Hyperledger.
learning_outcomes: Design and deploy smart contracts on Ethereum using Solidity
Build decentralized applications with Web3.js integration
Implement consensus mechanisms including PoW and PoS
Evaluate blockchain platforms for enterprise use cases
subjects.industry_relevance: Blockchain technology is disrupting finance, supply chain, identity management, and digital asset markets.

subjects.name: DevOps Engineering
subjects.description: Advanced DevOps practices covering infrastructure as code, configuration management, container orchestration, CI/CD pipelines, monitoring, logging, and site reliability engineering principles.
learning_outcomes: Manage containerized applications using Docker and Kubernetes
Implement infrastructure as code using Terraform and CloudFormation
Build comprehensive CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and Jenkins
Set up monitoring and observability using Prometheus and Grafana
subjects.industry_relevance: DevOps practices are adopted by virtually every tech company to accelerate software delivery and operational reliability.

subjects.name: Site Reliability Engineering
subjects.description: Study of SRE principles including service level objectives, error budgets, incident management, capacity planning, chaos engineering, and reliability patterns for large-scale distributed systems.
learning_outcomes: Define and measure Service Level Objectives and error budgets
Implement incident management and postmortem processes
Conduct chaos engineering experiments to validate system resilience
Design capacity planning models for distributed systems
subjects.industry_relevance: SRE practices are essential for maintaining reliability of services at companies like Google, Netflix, and Amazon.

subjects.name: Ethical Hacking
subjects.description: Comprehensive ethical hacking course covering reconnaissance, vulnerability assessment, exploitation, web application security, wireless attacks, social engineering, and defensive countermeasures.
learning_outcomes: Perform network reconnaissance and vulnerability scanning
Exploit web application vulnerabilities following OWASP methodology
Conduct wireless security assessments and social engineering engagements
Implement defensive measures and security hardening techniques
subjects.industry_relevance: Ethical hackers are crucial for proactive security testing and vulnerability discovery across all industries.

subjects.name: Robotic Process Automation
subjects.description: Study of RPA technologies for automating repetitive business processes. Covers RPA tools like UiPath and Automation Anywhere, process identification, bot development, and enterprise RPA deployment.
learning_outcomes: Identify suitable processes for robotic automation
Develop automation bots using UiPath or Automation Anywhere
Implement attended and unattended automation workflows
Manage enterprise RPA deployment including orchestrator and robot fleet
subjects.industry_relevance: RPA is transforming business operations in finance, healthcare, insurance, and logistics with significant cost and efficiency gains.

subjects.name: Bioinformatics
subjects.description: Interdisciplinary field combining biology, computer science, and statistics. Covers genomic sequence analysis, protein structure prediction, phylogenetic analysis, and biological databases.
learning_outcomes: Analyze DNA and protein sequences using bioinformatics tools
Perform genome assembly and annotation
Construct phylogenetic trees from molecular data
Query biological databases using APIs and SPARQL
subjects.industry_relevance: Bioinformatics is critical for drug discovery, personalized medicine, genomics research, and biotechnology innovation.

subjects.name: Computational Linguistics
subjects.description: Study of computational approaches to human language including syntax, semantics, discourse analysis, corpus linguistics, statistical NLP, and machine translation systems.
learning_outcomes: Apply formal grammars for syntactic parsing
Implement semantic analysis and discourse processing systems
Build statistical language models from large corpora
Develop machine translation systems using neural approaches
subjects.industry_relevance: Computational linguistics powers voice assistants, search engines, translation services, and content analysis platforms.

subjects.name: Cognitive Science
subjects.description: Interdisciplinary study of mind and intelligence combining psychology, neuroscience, AI, philosophy, and linguistics. Covers perception, memory, reasoning, decision making, and consciousness.
learning_outcomes: Explain cognitive architectures and models of memory and attention
Analyze neural correlates of cognitive processes
Design cognitive models using computational frameworks
Evaluate AI systems from cognitive science perspectives
subjects.industry_relevance: Cognitive science informs AI development, user experience design, brain-computer interfaces, and educational technology.

subjects.name: Human-Computer Interaction
subjects.description: Study of design, evaluation, and implementation of interactive computing systems. Covers usability heuristics, user research, interaction design, prototyping, accessibility, and user experience evaluation.
learning_outcomes: Apply user-centered design processes for interactive systems
Conduct user research including interviews, surveys, and usability testing
Design and prototype interfaces using Figma or similar tools
Evaluate interface usability using heuristic evaluation and cognitive walkthroughs
subjects.industry_relevance: HCI expertise is essential for product design teams creating intuitive and accessible digital products across all platforms.
